paper_title: Biostratigraphic and Morphological Analysis of Diatom Species in the Bering Sea

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing sediment samples from Core U1340A for morphological and taxonomic analysis.

steps.description: Samples were collected from Core U1340A, which was recovered from the southeastern flank of the Bowers Ridge at 1295m water depth.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from Core U1340A

steps.description: A reduced amount of 0.3g of wet sediment was processed for SEM observations using a washing technique and 8-hour settling process until samples reached pH ~7.
steps.searchable_summary: process sediment samples for SEM by washing and settling

steps.description: Sieving was performed to concentrate the target species and eliminate the valves and debris smaller than 15μm.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve sediment samples to concentrate target species

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the morphological variations and taxonomic criteria of diatom species P. barboi and P. curvirostris.

steps.description: Morphologic variations of P. barboi and P. curvirostris were observed and studied through SEM imaging.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze morphological variations using SEM imaging

steps.description: The width and curvature of the proboscis fragments were used as the main taxonomic criteria to distinguish both species.
steps.searchable_summary: measure width and curvature of proboscis fragments for taxonomic analysis

methods.description: Analysis of the stratigraphic distribution of P. barboi and P. curvirostris to understand their occurrence and potential as biostratigraphic markers.

steps.description: Analyze the stratigraphic distribution of P. barboi and P. curvirostris at Site U1340 and Site U1341.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stratigraphic distribution of diatom species

steps.description: Compare the stratigraphic distributions to environmental conditions and propose potential explanations for observed patterns.
steps.searchable_summary: compare stratigraphic distributions to environmental conditions
